summary: Add the typed BFF contract and durable stream projection for the React Agent Workbench rebuild.
rationale: The React workbench must consume a stable BFF contract instead of runtime/provider payloads. This slice intentionally touches BFF routes, projection, durable stream storage, and the minimum conversation-agent store/model fields required for replay, ordering, and lifecycle pairing.
line_count_rationale: The pre-existing Workbench API regression file is already over the test-file budget. This PR adds four focused assertions for BFF contract behavior without splitting unrelated historical coverage in the same contract slice.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
deletion_targets: Unpaired tool/runtime lifecycle events in the public workbench stream
risks: BFF DTOs must stay explicit and must not add object/list[object]/unknown payload sinks.
Durable stream replay must preserve stable sequence ordering across reconnects.
Conversation-agent model/store additions must remain minimal backend facts, not UI presentation fields.
